Author: Stanford Law School
Title: CodeX | Stanford Law School
Text:
Legal Empowerment
through Information Technology
CodeX is a global epicenter for research and development of computational law. As a multidisciplinary center sitting between Stanford Law School and the Stanford Department of Computer Science, our mission is to advance the frontier of legal technology, driving efficiency, transparency, and access within legal systems.
Our research centers focus on three core initiatives, each aimed to advance foundational academic insights and practical applications.
Recent News
Recent Publications
CodeX FutureLaw Week 2026
Agentic AI & Law Bootcamp | April 11-12, 2026
Hands-on accelerated program for interdisciplinary Stanford student teams designing and prototyping agentic AI applications at the intersection of technology and law.
LLM x Law Hackathon | April 12, 2026
A hackathon uniting AI innovators, machine learning specialists, and legal professionals to prototype the future of legal technology.
UN AI For Good – Law Track | April 15, 2026
An international conference uniting leading legal experts, policymakers, and business leaders to harness AI for sustainable, ethical, and responsible legal systems.
FutureLaw Conference |April 16, 2026
FutureLaw 2026 brings together global leaders in law, technology, and policy to explore how computational law and legal AI can answer these challenges.
Codex Prize
The CodeX Prize is an annual award given to an individual or individuals for a noteworthy contribution to Computational Law – an idea, article, book, computer application, computer tool, organization, etc. that has had significant and enduring positive impact on the field.
Learn More : Codex Prize
